title: Bridge Paper: Mindfulness-induced endogenous theta stimulation occasions self-transcendence an
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Emptiness** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Emptiness — dissolution of self, ego, DMN decoupling, non-self in neuroscience.
**Phase context:** ego dissolution, self-loss, self-transcendence, depersonalization, default mode network

Paper: Mindfulness-induced endogenous theta stimulation occasions self-transcendence and inhibits addictive behavior.
DOI: 10.1126/sciadv.abo4455
Abstract: Self-regulation is instantiated by theta oscillations (4 to 8 Hz) in neurons of frontal midline brain regions. Frontal midline theta (FMΘ) is inversely associated with default mode network (DMN) activation, which subserves self-referential processing. Addiction involves impaired self-regulation and DMN dysfunction. Mindfulness is an efficacious self-regulatory practice for treating addiction, but little is known about the mechanisms by which mindfulness reduces addictive behavior. In this mechan
